summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting focused heavily on tornado recovery efforts from the July 6th storm, accepting multiple grants and donations, and approving various departmental purchases. Key discussions included updates on the damaged Station 18 fire building (estimated 2-3 year rebuild timeline), ARP fund allocations totaling approximately $1.4 million, approval of LED monument signs, a new police K-9, and the creation of a new zoning inspector position. Duke Energy presented a $10,000 check to help replace items lost in the tornado.
decisions: Adopted the meeting agenda
Approved payment of bills from August 17 through September 13, 2022
Approved resolution R043-2022 denying major adjustment to Cove of Barclay Woods PUD for 1,366 sq ft dwelling (corrected with rescind and re-vote)
Accepted Home Depot grant of $2,217 for cleaning supplies post-tornado
Accepted Duke Energy grant of $10,000 for tornado recovery
Accepted $50 La Rosa gift card from Steve Short
Accepted FEMA Assistance to Firefighters Grant (AFG) of $57,142.85 for paramedic training and rescue tools
Increased revenue in fire fund line item 2111-511-2111 by $28,500
Increased appropriations in fire fund line item 2111-511-430-0000 by $35,000 for AFG tools and equipment
Accepted donation of K-9 Kirker from St. Michael Shepherds LLC for Officer Bucksath
Approved retirement and sale of K-9-6 to Officer Bucksath for $1
Accepted $400 in gift cards from CPAAA
Appropriated $41,680 for motor vehicles in police fund line item 2081-760-750-0000
Approved purchase of 2023 Ford Explorer police interceptor from Lebanon Ford for $41,680
Accepted resignation of part-time administrative assistant Jennifer Farmer effective September 29, 2022
Approved resolution R-051-2022 accepting amounts and rates from budget commission and certifying tax levies
Appropriated $100,000 in ARP fund for two LED monument signs
Approved enterprise zone agreement amendment for Brian Equipment
Approved job description for zoning inspector/plan review position and authorized posting
Rescinded 6-28-22 approval increasing $50,000 in road and bridge fund for road paving
Increased appropriations $50,000 in gas tax fund for road paving
Adjourned into executive session at 8:11 PM to discuss employment compensation
Adjourned regular meeting at 9:30 PM

Administrator to post and advertise the zoning inspector/plan reviewer position
Steve to follow up with county building department on Station 18 code determination within one week and report back to trustees

Chief Myers to plan awards ceremony for October 13 at 6:30 PM at golf course
Chief Rose to plan retirement ceremony for K-9-6
Insurance claims to be pursued for damaged signs at Station 18 and township building

attendees: Victoria McCauley-McGill (public speaker, Urbana Terrace resident)
notes: Meeting included correction of a double-negative motion on item C (Barclay Woods PUD denial) which required rescinding and re-voting. Three firefighters (Willie Burns, Rick Holland, Captain Robert Rose) reached 25-year service milestones. The fiscal officer was unable to attend the meeting. Executive session lasted from 8:11 PM to 9:27 PM with no decisions. Trustee Hodges abstained on the Brian Equipment enterprise zone agreement vote. The tornado referenced throughout was an F2/F3 that directly hit Station 18 on July 6, 2022. New service truck confirmed off assembly line and expected at dealer this week. Holly Fair community event upcoming. National Tradesmen Day noted for the following Friday.
